name: Potato Farls (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and halve the potatoes; place them in a large saucepan with enough water to cover.
2. Bring to a boil, then simmer, covered, for 20 to 30 minutes, or until fork-tender.
3. Drain well. Return the potatoes to the saucepan over low heat. Add butter and mash the potatoes thoroughly.
4. Stir in flour and salt. Gather the mixture into a ball; turn onto lightly floured surface.
5. Knead lightly until smooth. Divide dough into half.
6. Roll out one portion of the dough into an 8 inch (20 cm) circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
7. Cut into quarters and set aside. Repeat with the remaining dough.
8. Heat half the oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
9. Cook the dough pieces in batches for 2 minutes on each side or until golden brown, adding more oil as necessary.
10. Serve warm.
